name: Cheese Soufflé Bread
recipeIngredient: 1 cup shredded Swiss or Cheddar cheese, or 1/2 cup
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large bowl, combine yeast with water and milk. Stir in sugar and let stand for 10-15 minutes until foamy.
2. Mix flour and salt in the bowl. Add the yeast mixture and oil. Stir to combine.
3. Beat egg on low speed until blended. Increase speed to medium and beat in egg. Continue beating for an additional 3 minutes, scraping sides of the bowl occasionally.
4. Stir in remaining flour, cheese, and garlic (or minced garlic). If the dough is sticky, add up to 2 tablespoons more flour. Cover and let rise in a warm place until doubled in size, about 45 minutes.
5. Grease a 1-quart casserole dish. Turn the dough out onto the prepared surface and shape into a round loaf. Place it in the casserole dish.
6. Brush top with softened margarine (or butter). Sprinkle with Parmesan cheese and cracked pepper.
7. Cover and let rise again until doubled, about 35 minutes.
8. Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C).
9. Bake until the loaf is golden brown and sounds hollow when tapped, about 30-35 minutes. If browning too quickly, loosely cover with aluminum foil during the last 15 minutes of baking.
10. Remove from the casserole dish and let cool on a wire rack. Serve warm.
